title: Turn a Column into Notes on a dashboard
excerpt: I want to take a long text column on a board and turn that information into Notes on a dashboard. WE are doing a survey and there is a written response question.  When I go into the dashboard and choose notes there is no…

posts.author: tkthieme (Tonya Thieme)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to take a long text column on a board and turn that information into Notes on a dashboard. WE are doing a survey and there is a written response question. When I go into the dashboard and choose notes there is no Setting setup to obtain information this way. Does anyone know if this is possible through an app?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you have multiple lines with text that you want to pull in? Could you use a table and only show that column?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @tkthieme You’re right—Monday.com’s native “Notes” widget on dashboards is limited and doesn’t allow you to directly pull in data from a long text column in a board. That widget is more for manual note-taking than dynamically displaying board content.

Here are a few workarounds and app-based options:

1. Use the “Table” Widget Instead (Native Option)

You can add a Table widget to your dashboard and configure it to show the board and the specific column (including long text responses).

  • Go to your dashboard
  • Add a widget > Table
  • Select your board and the long text column
  • It’s not as “note-like,” but it does display the survey responses in a clean, readable way.

2. Use a Third-Party App

There are apps in the Monday Apps Marketplace that can help make this data more readable or displayed as “notes”:

  • DocuGen or Form Viewer might work for formatting long text fields into a more readable, document-style format.
  • Apps like General Caster or Make can also be used to push this data elsewhere (like a Google Doc, PDF, or another board) that you could then reference on your dashboard.

3. Use an Automation + Integration

If you’re comfortable with tools like Make or Zapier, you can:

  • Watch the board for new survey responses
  • Send those long text entries to another system (like Google Docs or Notion)
  • Embed or link that output into the Monday dashboard using the Link or Embed widget.

If this helps let me know or ask for more support!

posts.author: tkthieme (Tonya Thieme)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I am using the table. But I wanted it to look more like a paragraph so I could send out to teams with final comments. thanks
